steps.title: Find the small height
steps.content:
In right triangle $ABC$, $AB=\sqrt{89}$ and $AC=8$. Use the Pythagorean theorem to find $BC$:
$$BC^2+8^2=(\sqrt{89})^2$$
$$BC^2+64=89$$
$$BC=5$$

steps.title: Scale to the larger triangle
steps.content:
The full base is $AE=AC+CE=8+24=32$. Since the triangles share the same slanted line, the height-to-base ratio is:
$$\frac{BC}{AC}=\frac{5}{8}$$
So the larger height is:
$$DE=\frac{5}{8}\cdot 32=20$$

steps.title: Compute the triangle area
steps.content:
Use the area formula for triangle $ADE$:
$$\frac{1}{2}(32)(20)=320$$
The answer is 320.
